title: Entity type — kind of object that can be collected into a set
description: Controlled vocabulary of entity types. Defines what KIND of thing a set contains. Each value in the enum is a canonical type; atlases may add their own (additionalProperties on set_v1.entity_type is allowed). Per SETS_AND_ANALYSES.md §'Entity types'.

entity_type.description: Canonical name. Lowercase + underscores. Singular noun (sample, not samples). The 'kind' of object.

description.description: One-line definition: 'what is one of these?'

coordinate_aware.description: True if instances carry chrom + start_bp + end_bp coordinates. Used by query builders to know whether a set can be filtered by region.

id_pattern.description: Optional regex hint for valid instance ids (e.g. variant_site uses 'chrom:pos:ref:alt').

examples.description: A few example instance ids for documentation.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Sample
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One sequenced individual. The fundamental unit of who-was-measured.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Variant site
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One position in the genome with at least two observed alleles. Typically biallelic SNPs in BEAGLE/sites contexts.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Genomic window
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One half-open coordinate interval [start_bp, end_bp). Bin for statistics (FST per window, theta per window, dosage per window).

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Interval
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One named coordinate range (candidate region, gene body, conserved block). Can be any size. Distinct from 'window' which is the bin-for-stats notion.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Inversion candidate
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One putative inversion call (born from D17, local-PCA, SV concordance). Has coordinates, lineage, evidence blocks.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Breakpoint
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One side of an SV junction. Has chrom + bp (single position).

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Gene
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One annotated gene (from GFF). Has coordinates + name.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Transposable element copy
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One TE insertion / fragment. Annotation source: RepeatModeler / EDTA.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Genetic marker
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One assay target (PCR primer pair, ddPCR probe, SNP genotype assay). Usually has coordinates and a target sequence.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Chromosome
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One reference chromosome / linkage group. Coordinate-aware only in the sense that it's the parent of all positions on it.

_canonical_vocabulary.label: Scaffold
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One scaffold in the genome assembly (may or may not be promoted to a chromosome).

_canonical_vocabulary.label: BAM file
_canonical_vocabulary.description: One per-sample alignment file. Sets of BAMs are typically one-to-one with sample sets, but registered separately so processing variants (markdup vs raw, downsampled vs full-depth) can be tracked.
